# test_palindrome.py
import pytest

from your_module import is_palindrome   #  <-- replace with the real import path


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Happy-path tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "text",
    [
        "racecar",
        "RaceCar",                        # mixed case
        "A man a plan a canal panama",    # internal spaces
        "Was it a rat I saw",
        "12321",                          # numeric characters in a string
    ],
)
def test_is_palindrome_true(text):
    """Known palindromes should return True."""
    assert is_palindrome(text) is True


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "text",
    [
        "hello",
        "palindrome",
        "Madam, I'm Adam",   # punctuation breaks the palindrome
        "A man a plan",      # incomplete sentence
    ],
)
def test_is_palindrome_false(text):
    """Non-palindromes should return False."""
    assert is_palindrome(text) is False


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Edge-case tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "text, expected",
    [
        ("", True),          # empty string
        (" ", True),         # string with only a space
        ("    ", True),      # multiple spaces
        ("x", True),         # single character
        ("X", True),         # single uppercase character
        ("Åå", True),        # unicode, case-insensitive
    ],
)
def test_is_palindrome_edge_cases(text, expected):
    """Edge cases: empty, single char, only spaces, unicode."""
    assert is_palindrome(text) is expected


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Error-case tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@pytest.mark.parametrize("bad_input", [123, 12.3, None, ["r", "r"], {"text": "rr"}])
def test_is_palindrome_type_error(bad_input):
    """
    The function expects a string.  Non-string inputs should raise AttributeError
    because they lack .lower() / .replace() methods.
    """
    with pytest.raises(AttributeError):
        is_palindrome(bad_input)
